FILM 175 -- Film and Culture \| Sp26

St. Cloud State University \| Professor Wiener

Student: Yohannes Nigusse

**Claude AI -- Course Assistance Context Document**

*This document is specific to FILM 175 Sp26 and is intended to give
Claude working context for this course only. It is not a general
reference.*

**1. What This Project Is For**

This Claude project has been set up specifically to assist Yohannes
Nigusse in FILM 175 -- Film and Culture, Spring 2026, taught by
Professor Wiener at St. Cloud State University. It is not a
general-purpose assistant. All help provided here is scoped to this
course --- its readings, assignments, quizzes, writing style, and
grading expectations.

Claude has been added to this project with full context from the course
materials. When answering questions or helping with work, Claude should
always draw from what has been established in this course, not from
general knowledge alone.

**2. Course Overview**

  --------------------- -------------------------------------------------
  **Course**            FILM 175 -- Film and Culture

  **Semester**          Spring 2026

  **Professor**         Professor Wiener

  **Platform**          D2L (Desire2Learn) --
                        stcloudstate.learn.minnstate.edu

  **Student**           Yohannes Nigusse (ho5536fs@go.minnstate.edu)
  --------------------- -------------------------------------------------

**3. Writing Style & Tone**

Claude has read Yohannes\'s submitted papers for this course and should
match his established academic voice in all written work. Key
characteristics:

-   Clear, direct, and analytical --- no filler or over-explanation

-   Define the concept first, explain what it does, then connect to the
    specific example

-   Confident declarative sentences --- academic but readable

-   Consistent structure: introduction → body analysis → conclusion

-   Specific timestamps and details cited when analyzing film

-   Never pads length --- thorough but concise

**4. Completed Assignments & Papers**

The following work has already been submitted this semester and is
available as context for Claude:

  -------------------------- ---------------------- ---------------------
  **Assignment**             **Topic**              **Date**

  Mise-en-Scène Paper        Beauty and the Beast   Feb 8, 2026
                             (1946) ---             
                             Frame-within-a-frame   

  Montage Paper              Battleship Potemkin    Feb 15, 2026
                             --- Five styles of     
                             montage                

  What Do They Do? Quiz      Unit 7 --- Production  Mar 4, 2026
                             roles + Singin\' in    
                             the Rain credits       
  -------------------------- ---------------------- ---------------------

**5. Course Materials Claude Has Read**

The following documents have been uploaded and read by Claude for this
course:

-   Making_A_Film_stages.docx --- The 5 stages of film production

-   PROCESS_AND_PEOPLE_TO_MAKE_A_FILM.docx --- People and roles at each
    production stage

-   PRODUCTION_DEPARTMENTS.docx --- Full department and job title
    breakdown

-   Tomaric-Chapter8-Crew_structure.pdf --- Visual crew hierarchy chart
    (small Hollywood film)

-   production for Film ARTS 1--18 (images) --- Bordwell & Thompson
    textbook, pp. 17--34

-   Yohannes_Nigusse_Mise-en-Scène.docx --- Submitted paper (writing
    style reference)

-   The_Battleship_Potemkin_Nigusse.docx --- Submitted paper (writing
    style reference)

**6. Key Credits --- Singin\' in the Rain (1952)**

These credits were researched and verified for Unit 7. Claude should use
these when connecting job roles to specific people on this film:

  -------------------------- --------------------------------------------
  **Role**                   **Person**

  Producer                   Arthur Freed

  Directors                  Gene Kelly & Stanley Donen

  Screenplay                 Betty Comden & Adolph Green

  Cinematographer (DP)       Harold Rosson

  Editor                     Adrienne Fazan

  Art Direction              Cedric Gibbons & Randall Duell

  Set Decoration             Edwin B. Willis & Jacques Mapes

  Costume Design             Walter Plunkett

  Music Direction            Lennie Hayton

  Choreography               Gene Kelly (& Stanley Donen, uncredited)

  1st AD                     Fred Datig Jr.

  Camera Operator            Frank V. Phillips
  -------------------------- --------------------------------------------

**7. Units Covered So Far**

For Claude\'s reference, the following units have been completed or are
in progress:

-   Unit 1 -- Visual Storytelling ✓

-   Unit 2 -- How It All Began: History of Image Making ✓

-   Unit 3 -- Mise-en-Scène ✓ (paper submitted)

-   Unit 4 -- Editing and Montage Theory ✓ (paper submitted)

-   Unit 5 -- Silent Storytellers ✓

-   Unit 6 -- We Hear It! Introduction of Sound ✓

-   Unit 7 -- The Production Process ← CURRENT (quiz in progress)

-   Unit 8 -- Genres (upcoming)

*This document is scoped to FILM 175 Sp26 only. Updated: March 4, 2026.*

# Courses & Skill Plan — Yohannes Nigusse
> Last updated: 2026-03-10

---

## AI Engineer Roadmap (6-Step)

| Step | Topic | Status | Notes |
|------|-------|--------|-------|
| 1 | Python for AI | DONE | Course completed. Confidence gained on workflow. |
| 2 | Design AI Systems | IN PROGRESS (via AI Aggregator) | Learning through Dave Ebbelaar tutorial + building Phase 2 |
| 3 | Production-Ready Backends (FastAPI, Docker, PostgreSQL) | IN PROGRESS (via AI Aggregator) | Database + pipeline work in Phase 2 |
| 4 | RAG — Connect AI to Data | DEFERRED | Post-NSBE deep dive |
| 5 | Monitor and Optimize AI Systems | DEFERRED | Post-NSBE |
| 6 | Ship to Production | DEFERRED (optional Render deployment) | Post-NSBE, optional |

**API Keys:** OpenAI and Anthropic both set up and working.

**Direction:** Learning Steps 2–3 in parallel via the AI Aggregator project. Real project context > isolated coursework.

---

## Active Learning: Dave Ebbelaar "Complete AI News Aggregator" Video

**Timeline:** Watching as guide for Phase 2 build (not blindly following)
**Effective focus:** 0:00–1:40:36 (Intro through Email Agent)
**Why:** Teaches full pattern: scrape → database → LLM → output → (optional: email/deployment)

| Timestamp | Section | Relevance to AI Aggregator |
|-----------|---------|---------------------------|
| 0:00–13:08 | Intro + brainstorming | Context on architecture decisions |
| 13:08–43:13 | YouTube + OpenAI + Anthropic scrapers, pipeline | **CORE** — scraper pattern + LLM integration |
| 43:13–1:16:28 | Database, markdown processing, testing | Database setup + persistence pattern |
| 1:16:28–1:40:36 | Digest + email agent | Optional — decide after seeing it |
| 1:40:36+ | Ranking, deployment, refactor | Defer or skip — post-NSBE |

**Approach:** Watch sections, build as you learn, make decisions on what's necessary for demo.

---

## CodePath AI110

10-week AI-native programming course. Currently Week 2 of 10. Runs through late April.

### Effort Triage

| Module | Weeks | Effort Level | What Matters |
|--------|-------|-------------|-------------|
| M1: AI-Native Programming Foundations | 1–2 | MINIMUM | Debugging guessing game with Copilot. Below Yohannes's level. Do it clean, move on. |
| M2: Systems & Algorithms (PawPal+) | 3–4 | MINIMUM | OOP, UML, pet scheduler. Checkbox work. |
| M3: ML Literacy (Music Recommender) | 5–6 | MODERATE | Weighted scoring, filter bubbles, model card. Mood Machine tinker worth genuine attention. |
| M4: Applied AI Systems (TeamMate) | 7–8 | DEEP | RAG, retrieval pipelines, guardrails. DocuBot tinker = real reps on defensible concepts. |
| M5: Agentic Workflows (Final Project) | 9–10 | DEEP | Agentic loops, risk assessment, human-in-the-loop. BugHound tinker. Final project = portfolio piece. |

### Tinker Labs Priority
- ByteBites (M2): Checkbox only
- Mood Machine (M3): Build classifier from scratch, explain why it fails — worth real engagement
- DocuBot (M4): DEEP — retrieval pipeline, guardrails, naive vs RAG comparison
- BugHound (M5): DEEP — agentic workflow, risk assessment, human-in-the-loop logic

### Key Point
CodePath final project (due late April) and NSBE demo are SEPARATE. Different timelines, different outputs. CodePath does not save NSBE.

---

## Spring 2026 Coursework

| Course | Target Grade | Strategy | Notes |
|--------|-------------|----------|-------|
| Intro to OS | A | Engaged | Genuinely interested. Expects an A. |
| AI / Neural Networks | A | Minimum deep engagement | AI handles projects. Not the priority — Aggregator project takes that role. |
| SE 482: Computer Animation | A | Engaged | Building Unity game using Claude Code in 1-on-1 sessions with professor. Genuine enjoyment. Working through scripts bottom-up. |
| Gen Ed (x3) | A | AI-assisted | Documentation finalization with AI assistance. |

**College strategy: Get As everywhere. Only go deep where it serves Applied AI Engineering or genuine interest.**

---

## Skill Development (Via AI Aggregator Phase 2)

By end of March 18:

**Defensible skills gained:**
- End-to-end project architecture (scrape → process → LLM → output)
- Database design + SQLAlchemy ORM
- LLM integration (Anthropic API)
- Error handling + graceful degradation (25 feeds, one bad feed doesn't break everything)
- Testing (pytest for scrapers)
- CLI design (clean main.py entrypoint)

**Can speak to:**
- Why Anthropic over OpenAI (cost, reliability, context window)
- Why iCal over HTML scraping (standardized format, less brittle)
- Database vs. in-memory trade-offs
- Summarization prompts (what works, what doesn't)
